mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: Sudden switch to responses from completions causing failures
mission.description: [Bug]: Sudden switch to responses from completions causing failures. This issue affects 19+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

PR #16766 was merged in at night, and models started using `responses` instead of `completions`. The bridge does not work automatically for many cases, and structured outputs also failed for these requests.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: Sudden switch to responses from completions causing…
steps.description: Versioning might be a good idea - to catch regressions, before rolling out to everyone on model prices. We already support pinning to a local copy via ```bash export LITELLM_LOCAL_MODEL_COST_MAP="True" ```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: Sudden switch to responses from… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Versioning might be a good idea - to catch regressions, before rolling out to everyone on model prices.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
